stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 4 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 10 observations with mean 41.1 % (within-hospital SD = 8.3). hospital B has 5 observations with mean 73.6 % (within-hospital SD = 4.7). hospital C has 8 observations with mean 74.6 % (within-hospital SD = 5.2). hospital D has 5 observations with mean 70.6 % (within-hospital SD = 5.5). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.9 and 14.9 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital B: 73.1 % (shrunk from raw 73.6). Range [72.3, 73.4] as between-hospital SD varies from 5.9 to 14.9. Pooled mean: 61.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 61.7 % (weighted average of 4 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=9.9: B = 0.957.
Interval [72.3, 73.4] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (73.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (61.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
